subject: Biology
title: Endosymbiosis: Merging for Life
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Endosymbiosis: Merging for Life.

hook.text: Your mitochondria have their own DNA—because they were once free-living bacteria that moved in permanently.

buildup.text: Lynn Margulis proposed that mitochondria and chloroplasts originated as engulfed bacteria that became symbionts.

discovery.text: Both organelles have double membranes, their own circular DNA, and replicate independently inside cells.

twist.text: Margulis was ridiculed for decades. Now endosymbiosis is one of the most important ideas in biology.

climax.text: Without this ancient merger, there would be no complex life—no plants, no animals, no fungi.

punchline.text: All complex life exists because two cells became one.

quiz.question: What were mitochondria originally?
options: Free-living bacteria that became permanent cell residents
Parts of the nucleus that split off
Viral particles that infected early cells
